DfENumber.DfENumber: A unique identifier assigned by the Department for Education to each school.

SchoolName.SchoolName: The official name of the school.

Year.Year: The academic year for which the admissions data is recorded.

Places.Places: The total number of places available for admission at the school.

PlaceAllocated.PlaceAllocated: The number of places that have been allocated to students.

1stPref.1stPref: The number of students who listed the school as their first preference.

2ndPref.2ndPref: The number of students who listed the school as their second preference.

3rdPref.3rdPref: The number of students who listed the school as their third preference.

4thPref.4thPref: The number of students who listed the school as their fourth preference.

5thPref.5thPref: The number of students who listed the school as their fifth preference.

Unplaced.Unplaced: The number of students who were not placed in any school.

SLE.SLE: The number of students admitted under Special Legislative Enactment criteria.

Catchment.Catchment: The number of students admitted based on living within the school's catchment area.

Sibling.Sibling: The number of students admitted due to having a sibling already attending the school.

Distance.Distance: The number of students admitted based on their proximity to the school.

OutsideSpecifiedArea.OutsideSpecifiedArea: The number of students admitted from outside the specified catchment area.

InsideSpecifiedArea.InsideSpecifiedArea: The number of students admitted from within the specified catchment area.

Religion.Religion: The number of students admitted based on religious affiliation criteria.

Feeder.Feeder: The number of students admitted from feeder schools.

Other.Other: The number of students admitted based on other criteria not specified above.

Total.Total: The total number of students considered for admission.

5thPref.5thPref: The number of students who listed the school as their fifth preference.

Unplaced.Unplaced: The number of students who were not placed in any school.

SLE.SLE: The number of students admitted under Special Legislative Entry criteria.

CAMIS.CAMIS: A unique identifier assigned to each restaurant by the New York City Department of Health.

DBA.DBA: The name under which the restaurant operates, also known as 'Doing Business As'.

BORO.BORO: The borough in which the restaurant is located, such as Manhattan, Brooklyn, or Queens.

BUILDING.BUILDING: The building number where the restaurant is situated.

STREET.STREET: The street address of the restaurant.

ZIPCODE.ZIPCODE: The postal zip code of the restaurant's location.

PHONE.PHONE: The contact phone number for the restaurant.

CUISINE DESCRIPTION.CUISINE DESCRIPTION: A description of the type of cuisine served at the restaurant.

ACTION.ACTION: The action taken by the health inspector during the inspection, such as issuing a violation or closing the restaurant.

VIOLATION CODE.VIOLATION CODE: A code representing a specific health code violation found during the inspection.

INSPECTION TYPE.INSPECTION TYPE: The type of inspection conducted, such as a routine inspection or a follow-up inspection.

NTA.NTA: The Neighborhood Tabulation Area (NTA) where the restaurant is located, used for demographic and statistical purposes.

BNF Code.BNF Code: The British National Formulary (BNF) code, which is a hierarchical classification system for medications and other healthcare products.

BNF Chapter.BNF Chapter: The highest level of the BNF classification, representing broad categories of medications.

BNF Section.BNF Section: The second level of the BNF classification, representing more specific categories within a chapter.

BNF Paragraph.BNF Paragraph: The third level of the BNF classification, representing even more specific subcategories within a section.

BNF Sub-Paragraph.BNF Sub-Paragraph: The fourth level of the BNF classification, representing the most specific subcategories within a paragraph.

ticket_id.ticket_id: A unique identifier for each 311 service request or ticket.

issue_type.issue_type: The category or type of issue reported by the caller.

council_district.council_district: The city council district associated with the location of the issue.

city.city: The city where the 311 call was made or the issue is located.

closure_reason.closure_reason: The reason provided for closing the ticket, if applicable.

case_title.case_title: A brief title or summary of the issue reported in the 311 call.

Maximum Duration of Contract (Months).Maximum Duration of Contract (Months): The maximum possible duration of the contract, including any extensions, in months.

Contract End Date.Contract End Date: The date on which the contract is scheduled to end without any extensions.

Ext. Applied to Contract.Ext. Applied to Contract: Indicates whether any extensions have been applied to the contract.

Maximum Extend Date.Maximum Extend Date: The latest date to which the contract can be extended.

Review Date.Review Date: The date on which the contract will be reviewed for potential modifications or renewals.

Estimated Annual Value.Estimated Annual Value: The estimated financial value of the contract per year.

Estimated Total Value.Estimated Total Value: The total estimated financial value of the contract over its entire duration.

Supplier Name.Supplier Name: The name of the supplier or vendor providing goods or services under the contract.

Supplier Type.Supplier Type: The category or type of the supplier, such as a business, individual, or organization.

Registered Number.Registered Number: The official registration number of the supplier.

Postcode.Postcode: The postal code of the supplier's registered address.

Council Directorate.Council Directorate: The department or division within the council that oversees the contract.

GEO.GEO: The geographical location where the activity took place.

Activity.Activity: The specific activity being measured, in this case, watching VCR movies.

Time spent.Time spent: The amount of time spent on the specified activity.

UOM.UOM: The unit of measurement for the time spent, such as hours or minutes.

Level.Level: The educational level of the program, such as undergraduate or graduate.

TAP Level of Study.TAP Level of Study: The specific level of study within the TAP program, which may correspond to different academic stages.

Sector Type.Sector Type: The type of educational institution sector, such as public, private, or proprietary.

TAP Sector Group.TAP Sector Group: The grouping of institutions based on their sector type for TAP purposes.

Recipient Age Group.Recipient Age Group: The age range of the TAP recipient.

TAP Financial Status.TAP Financial Status: The financial status of the recipient as determined by TAP eligibility criteria.

TAP Award Schedule.TAP Award Schedule: The schedule or timing of the TAP award disbursements.

TAP Degree or NonDegree.TAP Degree or NonDegree: Indicates whether the recipient is enrolled in a degree-seeking or non-degree program.

BIC NUMBER.BIC NUMBER: A unique identifier assigned to each business or entity within the wholesale market system.

ACCOUNT NAME.ACCOUNT NAME: The official name of the account holder or business entity registered with the wholesale market.

TRADE NAME.TRADE NAME: The name under which the business operates or is known to customers and partners.

ADDRESS.ADDRESS: The physical address of the business or entity's main location or headquarters.

POSTCODE.POSTCODE: The postal code associated with the business's address, used for mail and delivery services.

PHONE.PHONE: The contact telephone number for the business or entity, used for communication purposes.

MARKET.MARKET: The specific wholesale market in which the business or entity operates or is registered.

APPLICATION TYPE.APPLICATION TYPE: The type of application submitted by the business or entity, such as new registration, renewal, or amendment.

Organisation Name.Organisation Name: The official name of the organization.

Organisation Type.Organisation Type: The type or category of the organization.

Payroll staff AO/AA Headcount.Payroll staff AO/AA Headcount: The total number of payroll staff at the Administrative Officer (AO) or Assistant Administrator (AA) grade.

Payroll staff EO Full-time Equivalent.Payroll staff EO Full-time Equivalent: The full-time equivalent (FTE) of payroll staff at the Executive Officer (EO) grade.

Payroll staff Grade 6/7 Full-time Equiva.Payroll staff Grade 6/7 Full-time Equiva: The full-time equivalent (FTE) of payroll staff at the Grade 6 or Grade 7 level.

Payroll staff SCS Headcount.Payroll staff SCS Headcount: The total number of payroll staff at the Senior Civil Service (SCS) grade.

Payroll staff SCS Full-time Equivalent.Payroll staff SCS Full-time Equivalent: The full-time equivalent (FTE) of payroll staff at the Senior Civil Service (SCS) grade.

Payroll staff Total Employees Headcount.Payroll staff Total Employees Headcount: The total number of all payroll staff employees.

Payroll staff Total Employees Full-time.Payroll staff Total Employees Full-time: The full-time equivalent (FTE) of all payroll staff employees.

Non-payroll Staff Agency staff (clerical.Non-payroll Staff Agency staff (clerical: The total number of non-payroll staff who are agency clerical or administrative workers.
The full-time equivalent (FTE) of non-payroll staff who are agency clerical or administrative workers.

Non-payroll Staff Intermin Managers Head.Non-payroll Staff Intermin Managers Head: The total number of non-payroll interim managers.

Non-payroll Staff Interim Managers Full-.Non-payroll Staff Interim Managers Full-: The full-time equivalent (FTE) of non-payroll interim managers.

Non-payroll Staff Specialist Contractors.Non-payroll Staff Specialist Contractors: The total number of non-payroll specialist contractors.
The full-time equivalent (FTE) of non-payroll specialist contractors.

Non-payroll Staff Consultants/Consultanc.Non-payroll Staff Consultants/Consultanc: The total number of non-payroll consultants or consultancy staff.
The full-time equivalent (FTE) of non-payroll consultants or consultancy staff.

Non-payroll Staff Total Employees Headco.Non-payroll Staff Total Employees Headco: The total number of all non-payroll staff employees.

Non-payroll Staff Total Employees Full-t.Non-payroll Staff Total Employees Full-t: The full-time equivalent (FTE) of all non-payroll staff employees.

Grand Total (workforce numbers) Headcoun.Grand Total (workforce numbers) Headcoun: The total headcount of all workforce members, including both payroll and non-payroll staff.

Grand Total (workforce numbers) Full-tim.Grand Total (workforce numbers) Full-tim: The full-time equivalent (FTE) of all workforce members, including both payroll and non-payroll staff.

Payroll Staff Costs Salary.Payroll Staff Costs Salary: The total salary costs for all payroll staff.

Payroll Staff Costs Allowances.Payroll Staff Costs Allowances: The total allowance costs for all payroll staff.

Payroll Staff Costs Non-consolidated per.Payroll Staff Costs Non-consolidated per: The total costs for non-consolidated performance payments for all payroll staff.

Payroll Staff Costs Overtime.Payroll Staff Costs Overtime: The total overtime costs for all payroll staff.

Payroll Staff Costs Employer pension con.Payroll Staff Costs Employer pension con: The total employer pension contributions for all payroll staff.

Payroll Staff Costs Employer national in.Payroll Staff Costs Employer national in: The total employer national insurance contributions for all payroll staff.

Payroll Staff Costs Total paybill for pa.Payroll Staff Costs Total paybill for pa: The total paybill for all payroll staff, including salary, allowances, performance payments, overtime, pension contributions, and national insurance contributions.

Non-payroll Staff Total cost of consulta.Non-payroll Staff Total cost of consulta: The total cost of consultants or consultancy services for non-payroll staff.

Non-payroll Staff Total non-payroll (CCL.Non-payroll Staff Total non-payroll (CCL: The total costs for all non-payroll (Contingent Contract Labour) staff.

Grand Total paybill/staffing (payroll an.Grand Total paybill/staffing (payroll an: The grand total of all paybill and staffing costs, including both payroll and non-payroll staff.
